name: Almond Legend Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle chopped almonds into a well-greased 10-inch Bundt pan; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, cream butter and shortening until smooth. Gradually add 1-1/4 cups sugar, beating well at medium speed of an electric mixer.
3. Add egg yolks, and beat well. Stir in lemon rind, juice, and flavorings; beat well.
4. In a separate bowl, combine flour, baking powder, soda, and salt.
5. Alternately add milk to the flour mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Mix after each addition. Set batter aside.
6. In a clean bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
7. Gently fold the beaten egg white mixture into the batter.
8. Pour batter into prepared pan and press a whole almond just below the surface of the batter.
9. Bake at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 50 to 55 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes before removing it. Transfer the cake to a wire rack and let cool completely.
11. Drizzle Apricot Glaze over the cooled cake. Yield: one 10-inch cake.
NOTE: Make Almond Legend Cake every New Year's Eve for a fun tradition at your house. Somewhere hidden in the baked cake will be a single whole almond. Whoever gets the almond in their slice of cake, according to legend, will enjoy good fortune during the year.
